examples: She is in her seventies, with curly white hair and a sweet, grandmotherly smile.
A small town prosecuting attorney called his first witness to a trial, a grandmotherly, elderly woman.
I watched him beating his bunch of grass against his knees, with that preoccupied grandmotherly air that elephants have.
Nievert's image as a grandmotherly caretaker of a public utility belies her real talent.
When a grandmotherly type strolled by, propped up by an assistant and a cane, she wound up lifting her walking stick and pointed it in my direction.
